etymology.description: 𠦝 (early form of 朝) + 月(月) (yuè, "moon") = semantic 𠦝 (morning) + phonetic 月 -> morning
etymology.interpretation: 朝(朝) (zhāo, "morning") visually represents the transition from night to day, capturing the concept of the morning when both the sun and the fading moon can be seen. Originally, it was an ideogrammic compound with components such as sun and moon to depict morning. In later forms, the character evolved into a phono-semantic compound with 𠦝 as the semantic component referring to dawn, and 月 月(yuè, "moon") contributing phonetic value, reflecting the combination of nighttime and daytime elements in the morning twilight.

examples.chinese: 每个早晨(每個早晨)，我都会去公园跑步(我都會去公園跑步)。
examples.pinyin: Měi ge zǎochén, wǒ dūhuì qù gōngyuán pǎobù.
examples.english: Every morning, I go jogging in the park.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在朝阳升起时(她喜歡在朝陽升起時)，喝杯热茶(喝杯熱茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài zhāoyáng shēng qǐ shí, hē bēi rè chá.
examples.english: She likes to drink a cup of hot tea when the morning sun rises.

examples.chinese: 今天早上(今天早上)，我起得很早(我起得很早)。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān zǎoshang, wǒ qǐ dé hěn zǎo.
examples.english: This morning, I got up very early.
